commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 17. "For Thou art the glory of their strength: and in Thy good pleasure Thou shall lift up our horns" (ver. 17): because it has seemed good to Thee, not because we are worthy.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Horn. Power and kingdom. (Worthington)
He speaks like a virtuous Levite, who acknowledges that all good came from the Lord. (Haydock)
He cannot speak of temporal blessings alone. (Berthier)
